description: CAPT-BAND — EQUIPMENT-SCOPED recipe for sgm-004: match on the equipment axis ALONE so the active-recipe band resolves regardless of the article. This closes a live-data gap: the REAL ERP (erpdb.llm_test_v3) keys orders by numeric article codes (e.g. 14-0204-003, 12-0044-002) that do NOT equal the demo article names (housing-A, bracket-B), and carries NO setup/mould column. The it-evaluator RecipeBandResolver picks the active production order on sgm-004 (status=in_arbeit, most-recent), resolves the band here by equipment, and delivers it to the edge on i3x.recipe.band.sgm-004. The article-specific recipes (recipe-sgm-004-pa66gf30-housing-a.json / -bracket-b.json) still win FIRST per ref when a demo article IS active (recipeId-sorted, first-match-wins); this default catches every real article. The bands mirror the housing-A SOLL (the documented part currently set up on sgm-004). Edit a band here + push -> live on the next it-evaluator refresh, no redeploy; the edge applies the delivered numbers literal-only. --- 2026-07-12 (v3.3.0), TWO CHANGES. (1) mouldTempC: SOLL 50 -> 85 degC, band [40,60] -> [80,90], toleranceSource process_estimate -> norm. The old 50 degC was simply not a PA66 setting: a PA66(-GF30) tool is tempered to 80-90 degC, below which the part does not crystallise properly. Because the number now comes from the resin's processing specification and not from someone's estimate, its toleranceSource is 'norm' = BINDING: this band may NOT be widened to make a deviation disappear. That is deliberate and it is the whole point — with the machine running 87.5 degC, virtually every measured value lands INSIDE [80,90] (Cp ~ 2.1, the recipe is perfectly holdable) while the process centre is off by 2.5 degC, i.e. Ca = |87.5-85|/5 = 0.50 against a policy ca_max of 0.125. The verdict the product must produce is therefore 'fix_reality' (re-centre the machine), never 'fix_charter' (widen the band). This is the SAME statement the part mass already makes (SOLL 10.35 / machine 10.40 / Ca 0.147) on a second, independent parameter: 100 % IN TOLERANCE — AND THE MACHINE STILL DEVIATES. The offset is deliberately small: a 35 degC miss would be worthless, every setter would spot it unaided. (2) peakPressureBar / holdPressureBar: the documented gate changes from inCycle==true to phaseName==HOLD, mirroring the corrected 'when' guards on recipe_peak_pressure_band / recipe_hold_pressure_band in the profile. The bands themselves ([450,560] / [300,380]) are UNCHANGED and were never wrong — the population they were being judged against was. See the profile constraints for the measurements.
